qualitative_claim: Tiny volume at a new swing low = last seller has sold; market reverts up once aggression goes silent.
bullish_rule: IsBarLow is new N-bar low AND green bar AND volume at the low price-level <= expVol AND one of next 1-2 bars trades > signal-bar high by >=1 tick.
bearish_rule: Mirror: new N-bar high, red bar, volume at high level <= expVol, next 1-2 bars trade < signal-bar low.
candidate_thresholds: expVol 3(crude)/5(FX)/9-10(rates,index); N=25 (alt 25/30/50); buffer=1 tick; window=2 bars
confirmation_rule: Next 1-2 bars break signal-bar extreme by >=1 tick
invalidation_rule: Next bar trades inside signal bar (no break in 2 bars); ~10-min sideways time stop
expected_edge: Core reversal trigger; he calls it his original+favorite, claims fast follow-through
notes: expVol is liquidity-scaled and drifts (single-digits->9-10): test dynamic low-percentile. [FORCED on single threshold]

qualitative_claim: His explicit A+: exhaustion FOLLOWING absorption at a level (prominent POC / value area not retraded).
bullish_rule: H01 bullish AND (bullish prominent POC near bar low OR value area present AND next bar range does NOT re-enter the value area).
bearish_rule: Mirror at swing high with bearish POC / value area.
candidate_thresholds: VA abandonment = next-bar range ∩ VA = empty (binary); POC at extreme
confirmation_rule: H01 confirm + VA/POC not retraded next bar
invalidation_rule: VA/POC retraded next bar; H01 invalidation
expected_edge: He labels value-area absorption 'A+' / 'highest confidence / French kiss'
notes: Key test of his A+ claim: does requiring absorption raise win-rate vs bare exhaustion?

qualitative_claim: 'Let the market take you in' - a print is only a trade if the next bar(s) extend beyond it.
bullish_rule: For any reversal candidate, require one of next 1-2 bars to break signal-bar extreme by >=buf before entry.
invalidation_rule: No break within window -> cancel candidate
expected_edge: He claims this gate is the difference between a pattern and a trade
notes: Compare expectancy: enter-on-signal-bar vs 2-bar follow-through. His strongest entry claim.

qualitative_claim: New/equal low on positive delta + footprint ratio defense = bread-and-butter reversal.
bullish_rule: New/equal N-low AND BarDelta>0 AND ratio<=0.69 (bounds-low) AND confirming green price action AND next bar trades higher.
invalidation_rule: Divergence alone (no ratio/price action) -> high failure; next bar inside
expected_edge: His most-repeated 'automatic' setup across 2016-2026
notes: Ratio formula UNDISCLOSED; reproduce from footprint+validate. 2017 cutoff ~7 differs (older def).

qualitative_claim: Price new extreme while cumulative delta does not = distribution/absorption warning (radar, not trigger).
bullish_rule: Price new N-low AND NinjaCVD not new low -> arm; require separate trigger (H01/H04) + follow-through.
candidate_thresholds: strict new extreme vs no-new-extreme on CVD
invalidation_rule: CVD confirms new extreme with price; no trigger appears
expected_edge: He uses CVD divergence as a heads-up, explicitly not a standalone entry
notes: Must be paired; alone it is weak. Confirmation-only per his words.

qualitative_claim: Three failed divergences/exhaustions in ~1 hr = trend day: stop fading, trade the breakout.
bullish_rule: If failed_divergence_count over trailing window >=3 -> suppress bullish/bearish fades; enable breakout-with.
expected_edge: Repeatedly stated; protects against the classic fade-into-trend blowup
notes: He REFUSES to hard-code the window. [FORCED] -> prefer consecutive-failure count over a clock.

qualitative_claim: A failed buy-stack then a sell-stack in a small zone (or opposite-color stack at extreme) = powerful reversal; go with the SECOND imbalance.
bullish_rule: BottomSellStackMax>=3 at a swing low (sell-imbs in green = absorption) OR failed buy-stack then sell-stack within <=5 ticks; bar closes beyond; not stale.
candidate_thresholds: imbRatio=4:1 (alt 2.5/3/3.5/10); imbVol~10 (era 50); stack>=3; zone<=5; decay<=5 bars
invalidation_rule: Same-color stack (continuation); stale (>5 bars); no close beyond
expected_edge: His clearest discrete reversal rule (opposing imbalance)
notes: imbVol is liquidity-scaled; same-color must be classified as continuation, not reversal.

qualitative_claim: Same-direction stacked imbalances are weak as reversals and usually mean continuation.
bullish_rule: Suppress bullish reversal if the stack at the low is BUY-imbalances in a green up-move (same color).
expected_edge: Avoids the common 'buy the stack' error he explicitly rebuts
notes: Direction-by-color is central to his imbalance reading.

qualitative_claim: 3+ non-consecutive imbalances in a bar fire ~3 pts earlier than stacked and are 'as important or more'.
bullish_rule: BuyImbalanceCount(or Sell)>=3 within one bar at a swing extreme + follow-through.
expected_edge: He claims earlier entry vs stacked (testable edge)
notes: Test the 'earlier/more profitable than stacked' claim directly.

qualitative_claim: Selling imbalances inside a green candle at a low = absorption/trapped sellers; shift signal, NOT squeeze fuel.
bullish_rule: Sell-imbalance(s) inside green bar at swing low AND next bar trades away (not back in).
bearish_rule: Mirror: buy-imbalances in red bar at high.
confirmation_rule: Next bar trades away from the bar (not back in)
invalidation_rule: Next bar trades back into the bar; treat trapped size as magnitude
expected_edge: Absorption read; he is emphatic trapped qty is small (a tell, not fuel)
notes: Do NOT scale conviction by trapped volume - explicit contrarian point.

qualitative_claim: Four bars of increasing delta magnitude = momentum signal; act on the 4th (strongest).
bullish_rule: 4 consecutive bars increasing |BarDelta| (positive) -> long on 4th, at/after a low.
expected_edge: Fully specified rule he states explicitly
notes: More a momentum/continuation tool; include for completeness.

qualitative_claim: Three+ bars of weakening |delta| (same sign) at an extreme = aggression dying ('water pressure').
bullish_rule: >=3 consecutive bars decreasing |BarDelta| (negative) into a swing low -> reversal arm + flip.
expected_edge: STARRED favorite (strength/weakness in context)
notes: [FORCED] strict monotone; try %-drop/slope with K in 2-4.

qualitative_claim: 'Strong' = final delta near its max and a high delta/volume ratio; confirms the new direction.
bullish_rule: After bullish trigger, require next-bar DeltaPercent>25% AND BarDelta>=0.95*MaxSeenDelta.
expected_edge: His quantified 'strong bar' definition
notes: [FORCED] 25%/95% are his examples; try z-score vs recent bars.

qualitative_claim: Read intrabar max/min delta, not just final: a bar swinging +max then closing strongly opposite = exhaustion.
bullish_rule: At a new low, MinSeenDelta very negative but close BarDelta>=0 (absorbed) -> bullish.
bearish_rule: At a new high, MaxSeenDelta very positive but close BarDelta<=0 -> bearish.
candidate_thresholds: large |intrabar extreme| vs near-0 or opposite close
invalidation_rule: Final delta agrees with the failed direction
expected_edge: Repeated 'read max/min not final' instruction
notes: Needs intrabar delta capture; available via MaxSeenDelta/MinSeenDelta.

qualitative_claim: Strong positive delta at a high with no continuation = trapped buyers = bearish (and mirror).
bullish_rule: Mirror: strong negative delta at a new low with no continuation -> bullish.
bearish_rule: New N-high AND high DeltaPercent positive AND next bar fails higher high / delta not sustained -> short.
confirmation_rule: Next bar fails to extend; delta fades
invalidation_rule: Buyers continue (higher high, sustained positive delta)
expected_edge: Counter-intuitive, well-explained, named setup
notes: Equivalent to 'delta divergence + no follow-through'.

qualitative_claim: Heavy volume that halts a move at the extreme = absorption wall; reverse when pressure removed.
bullish_rule: Volume at the low level >= svPct of bar volume AND price fails to extend next bar AND decent absolute volume.
expected_edge: Long-standing concept; threshold is a tool default
notes: [FORCED] 30% is a default, not a stated edge.

qualitative_claim: Prominent POC = line in the sand; valid only if next bar does not trade back into it.
bullish_rule: Bullish PPOC near bar low at swing low AND next bar does not re-enter POC zone -> long.
candidate_thresholds: POC at extreme; not retraded next bar (binary)
invalidation_rule: Next bar trades back through POC (invalidated)
expected_edge: Repeated 'untested next bar' validity rule
notes: POC-on-extreme obsolete on e-mini; use prominent POC.

qualitative_claim: Value area + POC both untraded on the next bar after balancing = high-confidence reversal/continuation level.
bullish_rule: Bullish VA at swing low AND next-bar range does not enter VA -> long; 2 consecutive abandoned VAs stronger.
expected_edge: Called 'highest confidence / favorite / gold signal'
notes: Needs robust value-area construction in NT.

qualitative_claim: A signal only counts at/within ~1 tick of a clean swing high/low or HOD/LOD after a real move; mid-move = skip.
bullish_rule: Require signal bar to be within tol ticks of a new N-bar swing extreme or HOD/LOD AND prior move >= moveMin.
feature_columns_needed: swing hi/lo (N); HOD/LOD; ATR; prior-move displacement
expected_edge: The single strongest discriminator he repeats
notes: moveMin 'big move into level' has NO stated number -> [FORCED]; sweep ATR-relative.

qualitative_claim: Exhaustion near 'unchanged'/open has no catalyst; right after major numbers prints are wonky.
bullish_rule: Suppress signals within c ticks of session open / prior close, and for M minutes after CPI/PPI/NFP/inventories.
expected_edge: Repeated stay-out rule; uses a literal 'unchanged' line
notes: Economic-calendar feed needed for the news part; 'unchanged' line is trivial.

qualitative_claim: A wide signal bar forces a wide stop and lowers quality ('scared of bars like this').
bullish_rule: Suppress/downgrade if signal-bar range > k*ATR.
expected_edge: Explicit setup-selection rule about stop feasibility
notes: Pure risk filter; tune k by instrument.

qualitative_claim: Stacked imbalances are immediate-term S/R only (~5 bars), NOT durable; stale ones mislead.
bullish_rule: Ignore imbalance/zone as active if older than decay bars.
expected_edge: Contradicts common 'lasting S/R' belief; he is explicit
notes: Cheap, high-value filter.

qualitative_claim: A single 1-tick stop-out does not invalidate the read; re-enter if order flow unchanged.
bullish_rule: After a stop-out with no change in delta/imbalance picture, allow ONE re-entry on a fresh follow-through.
expected_edge: Repeated management rule (tight stop + re-enter)
notes: Backtest as a management overlay; measures whether re-entry adds expectancy.

setup_type: Composite A+ score vs realized MFE
qualitative_claim: Confluence stacking ranks setup quality (A+ > A > B), though he says it is not pure arithmetic.
bullish_rule: Compute gated weighted score (file 02 sec 3); test monotonic relationship of grade with realized MFE/MAE.
expected_edge: Validates whether his verbal tiers map to a usable score
notes: [FORCED] biggest simplification: he says '6 plots != buy'. Treat as ranking, validate empirically.
